source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
A square is split into 4 smaller squares. All small squares should either be coloured in white or black. How many ways are there to colour the big square? (patterns are the same if they can be - as shown in the picture transformed into one another by rotation.)
<image1>

image_dependency: question_text explicitly references rotation equivalence 'as shown in the picture'
requires_image=true and text_dominant=false in the sample fields
asset_registry_record shows one valid image asset and registry_passed=true
the visual partition of the square and the intended rotation examples are likely supported by the image, though the 2x2 split is also partly described in text
multi_step: solving requires counting black/white colorings of 4 cells under rotational equivalence, which involves combining case analysis with symmetry handling
the task is not simple lookup; it requires at least enumeration plus grouping by rotations
metadata.multi_solution_policy.should_push_multi_solution_agent=true suggests stable reasoning depth / alternative solution potential
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text is a single explicit value: 6
the result is checkable by exhaustive enumeration of 2^4 colorings and collapsing by rotational symmetry
asset_registry_record has answer_present=true and registry_passed=true, so the sample is structurally complete for downstream checking

decision_reasons: 资产完整：题干、答案和主图像均存在，且 registry_passed=true、issues 为空。
综合质量较高：image_dependency_score=0.8、multi_step_score=0.68、verifiability_score=0.93，具备较强可验证性和一定推理深度。
答案为明确数字，且可通过枚举与旋转同构归并进行校验，适合进入候选池。
虽有“图像可能部分仅作示意”的风险标记，但题目并非缺少核心语义支撑，不足以降为 reject 或明显压低优先级。
